title: Quality-weighted coverage floor: covered status requires depth x verification x source-independence above threshold (breadth cannot be bought cheaply)
context: PLAN.md v0.2 replaces the binary covered/partial/missing ledger status with a quality-weighted coverage model: a node is marked 'covered' only if its depth x verification x source-independence composite score exceeds a configured quality floor. Below the floor, the node stays 'partial' regardless of how many sources or claims are attached. This closes the 'breadth bought cheaply' attack: without a quality floor an operator could race to a superficially wide taxonomy of thousands of shallow depth-1 nodes, each with one low-quality source, and claim the corpus is covered. The quality floor makes 'covered' mean something defensible. This is an M1 subsystem that modifies the ledger schema (atltuae-ledger-005) and the saturation gate (atltuae-gates-007).
objective: Implement quality-weighted coverage in the coverage ledger (packages/core/ledger) and connect it to the saturation gate (packages/core/gates): define a composite quality score = depth_score x verification_score x source_independence_score for each ledger node; configure a quality floor threshold (governance-set, parameterized); a node is marked 'covered' only if its composite score exceeds the floor — otherwise it stays 'partial'; the saturation gate's 'no open frontier nodes' condition is satisfied only by nodes above the quality floor; the per-cycle coverage ledger emits quality scores alongside status so the floor is transparent and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: A composite quality score (depth_score × verification_score × source_independence_score) is computed for each ledger node after each cycle; each component is documented with its operational definition and data source

A configurable quality floor threshold (governance-set, with the default value documented and rationale recorded) determines whether a node is 'covered' or 'partial'; a node with a composite score below the floor is always 'partial' regardless of raw claim or source count

The saturation gate (atltuae-gates-007) is updated to require that 'no open frontier nodes' counts only nodes above the quality floor — a 'partial' node is treated as an open frontier node for saturation purposes
The per-cycle coverage ledger emits per-node quality scores (and each component) alongside the covered/partial/missing status so the quality floor's effect is transparent and can be audited

A test confirms that a node populated with many shallow/low-quality sources stays 'partial' (quality floor not met) and does not satisfy the saturation gate, while a node with deep, verified, independent sources crosses to 'covered'
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer + maintainer (recorded)
